sourceMovie.title: Ocean's Eleven
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Ocean's Eleven (2001) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Now You See Me
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ocean's Eleven clicked for you, Now You See Me (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Reservoir Dogs

faq.question: What should I watch after Ocean's Eleven?
faq.answer: Try Now You See Me, Reservoir Dogs, The Usual Suspects—each shares DNA with Ocean's Eleven while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ocean's Eleven?
faq.answer: Ocean's Eleven (2001) is best described as Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ocean's Eleven part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ocean's Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
